title: Glassy-Jelly Energizing Algerian Pomegranate Tagine
description: A traditional Algerian tagine recipe with a twist of pomegranate molasses and a Buddhist temple-friendly spin, featuring a buttery and glassy-jelly sauce.
instructions: In a small bowl, mix together pomegranate molasses, almond butter, coconut sugar, and 1 tablespoon of ghee to make the glaze.
In a large clay tagine or Dutch oven, heat 2 tablespoons of ghee over medium heat.
Add the sliced red onions and cook until they're softened and lightly caramelized, about 20 minutes.
Add the grated ginger and cook for another minute.
Add the cinnamon, turmeric, and salt, and stir to combine.
Add the pistachios and cook for 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Transfer the tagine to the oven and bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened and the flavors have melded together.
Meanwhile, toast the pomegranate seeds in a small skillet over medium heat until fragrant and lightly browned.
Stir in the fresh mint and pomegranate molasses glaze.
Serve the tagine hot, topped with toasted pomegranate seeds and fresh mint leaves.
Let the dish rest for 10-15 minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld together.
